page: Amazon Web Services inf1.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the inf1 family, powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8275CL processor running at 2.5 GHz, configured with 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 35 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not feature discrete GPUs. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core and memory performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU test scores 1491.39, which is in the average category, while the multi-core score drops to a weak 3929.66. Similarly, Geekbench yields an average single-core score of 1149.0 but a weak multi-core score of 2526.0. Memory bandwidth is generally weak, with read-only large block transfers at 27.68 GB/sec and write large block transfers at 23.83 GB/sec. Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations also fall into the weak performance tier.

For specialized workloads, the server demonstrates average performance in text processing and single-threaded compression tasks, such as gzip compression at 25.33 GB/sec. LLM inference benchmarks using a small 135M model show an average text generation speed of 75.74 tokens/sec, though prompt processing is weaker at 225.83 tokens/sec. Given the dedicated CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server offers an entry-level compute-focused profile. It is best suited for lightweight machine learning inference tasks using small models, basic text processing, and single-threaded utility tasks where dedicated CPU execution is required without massive memory or local storage overhead.
description: Amazon Web Services inf1.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-focused instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 4 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance does not include local storage or discrete GPUs, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1491.39 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1149.0, alongside average text processing and small-model LLM text generation. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks are relatively weak. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for entry-level dedicated compute tasks, making it suitable for lightweight machine learning inference with small models, single-threaded processing, and basic web serving workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services inf1.xlarge is an x86_64 instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, optimized for entry-level machine learning inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services inf1.xlarge features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth for lightweight inference workloads.
tagline: An entry-level, compute-focused instance with dedicated Intel Xeon processors and balanced single-core performance for lightweight machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 4 vCPUs and 2.5 GHz clock speed
8.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage
Average single-core performance and text processing capabilities
Suited for lightweight machine learning inference and single-threaded workloads
